storyTitle: Add AI ennemy snake (one for now, but maybe more later)

parsedArtifact.summary: - Add enemy-aware gameplay in `snake/src/game.rs`, keep controls and the main tick loop unchanged, extend board rendering for enemy head/body, and update Snake docs/spec text. Existing dependencies appear sufficient.
findings: `snake/src/game.rs` currently models only one snake: `VecDeque<Point>` plus one direction/pending turn, one score, one food, and `Cell::{Empty, Head, Body, Food}`.
`snake/src/main.rs` advances gameplay through `Game::step(...)` on each running tick, so enemy movement can stay inside game logic without changing terminal or input flow.
`snake/src/input.rs` already matches the required controls; no input changes are needed.
`snake/src/render.rs` renders from the `Cell` model in the compact board-first layout, so enemy visuals can be added without changing the layout structure.
`snake/Cargo.toml` already includes `rand`, `ratatui`, and `crossterm`; no new dependency is expected.

`snake/README.md`, `doc/spec/ui.md`, and `doc/spec/overview.md` still describe solo play, and `doc/spec/overview.md` also contains stale “unchanged gameplay” / older layout wording that should be corrected while adding enemy behavior.
affectedFiles: `snake/src/game.rs` — shared snake state, enemy AI, collision resolution, food spawning, and unit tests.
`snake/src/main.rs` — minimal follow-up only if `Game` APIs change.
`snake/README.md` — describe enemy behavior, shared food, and collision outcomes.
`doc/spec/overview.md` — update broad Snake spec and remove stale solo-only wording.
parsedArtifact.implementationApproach:
1. Factor the single-snake state in `game.rs` into a small reusable snake model so player and enemy share body/direction helpers, while still keeping only one enemy field for this story.
2. Expand board cell reporting to distinguish player head/body, enemy head/body, food, and empty cells.
3. Add enemy initialization on a deterministic non-overlapping start position, and refactor occupancy helpers so food spawning always excludes both snakes.
4. Extract a deterministic enemy move chooser that:
   - evaluates wrapped movement toward food,
   - rejects immediately fatal moves,
   - falls back through a fixed safe-direction order for simple tests.
5. Rework `Game::step(...)` to resolve player and enemy moves from the same tick snapshot, so self-collision, player/enemy overlap, and head-to-head cases are handled consistently instead of by update order.
6. Preserve current scoring and tick-speed behavior by awarding score only on player food; enemy food only grows the enemy and respawns food.
7. Update rendering styles/symbols so the enemy is clearly visible in ready, running, and game-over states without changing the compact layout.
8. Add or extend unit tests for distinct initialization, enemy AI preference/fallback, wraparound, food spawning, enemy growth, and fatal collision scenarios.
risks: Simultaneous movement rules are the main risk: head-to-head, head-swap, and tail-vacate cases can become inconsistent if resolved sequentially.
Two snakes plus food require more free space than the current solo game; very small board sizes may need a guard or clearer assumptions.
Spec text is partly stale already, so documentation changes must avoid preserving outdated solo-only or sidebar-layout wording.
validation: ready and game-over screens still show player, enemy, and food distinctly,
enemy moves every running tick and chases food with safe fallbacks,
enemy food grows the enemy without changing player score or tick speed,
food never appears on either snake,
enemy-related fatal collisions end the run,
compact layout and resize warning still work.
